Ours is BOTH slower AND more memory-hungry than Liger (2412 vs 972MB) — our CE is the worst of the CE kernels. MoE per-expert remains sole time-win. CCE removed (worst).

note: baseline=lifted compiled CE (ce_compiled.py). .float() (C,V) fp32 buffer is the memory+latency culprit

change: fused forward: online-softmax+target-gather Triton kernel, read fp16 + fp32-accum in registers; removed .float() (C,V) buffer + torch.logsumexp + .gather (3 passes -> 1)
verdict: KEEP (local). mem-parity latency 1.88x->1.44x; 64MB now 1.31x less mem. await T4 ce_fit confirm
iter1 CONFIRMED on T4: fwd now 0.88-0.95x (was 0.39x), mem 2.35x less @384MB (was 1.32x). Gap now 100% backward (0.38-0.48x). PROMOTE.

change: gw.add_(mm(g.t(),hc)) instead of gw += mm(...).float() — removes 166MB fp32 (V,H) temp per chunk (cast fused into add_)
verdict: KEEP (marginal at proxy V=32000; temp only 65MB here, expect larger win on T4 V=81000 = 166MB x7chunks). await T4 confirm

change: #3: expose one-shot/parity budget (chunk==N) in bench + eval + doc the latency<->memory dial in fused_linear_cross_entropy docstring
verdict: KEEP. one-shot hits the 1.33x fwd+bwd floor (fwd tied + bwd 3-GEMM). budget = the memory<->latency dial; no single right value. await T4 ce_fit (expect oneshot ~1.33x = ~245ms at ~2.65GB).

finding: #3 one-shot REFUTED on T4: bwd identical across chunk counts (oneshot 249ms == 1GB 253ms == 384MB 252ms). Chunk overhead is NOT the bottleneck — the recompute GEMM is. one-shot peak 3421MB > compiled 3072 for zero speed gain -> DOMINATED by 384MB. Corrected docstring. Lesson: bwd is recompute-GEMM-bound, motivating #1.

change: bwd_mode='int8': fwd quantizes (chunk,V) logits per-row to int8 + scale via FUSED kernels (_fwd_reduce_q tracks absmax, _quant_kernel writes int8 — no torch temps); bwd DEQUANTIZES (_dequant_grad_kernel) instead of recompute GEMM -> 3 GEMM drops to 2
verdict: KEEP (local): int8 1.35x->1.09x, NEARLY TIES compiled, grad PASS, NaN-free. proxy V small so mem not yet shown; T4 V=81000 int8 q_all=1.3GB vs compiled 2.65GB ~= 2x less. AWAIT T4.

DISCARD (dominated). int8 only 10% faster (362->326) but 2x WORSE memory (1305->2633, holds (N,V) int8 1.3GB) -> VIOLATES keep-memory constraint. fwd +33ms (2nd (N,V) pass for per-row scale) ate the -69ms bwd GEMM win. Proxy V=32000 overstated (held int8 only 131MB there). Kept opt-in, not default.

change: added ref backend (dump recipe: cuDNN conv + torch glue, UNCOMPILED, pure-autograd) + readonce backend (fused conv+sigmoid with H-outer/K-inner loop reorder for L1/L2 reuse of the K overlapping taps); dropped cublas from default sweep (dominated)
local_signal: readonce FWD slower than tldot (1.394 vs 1.007ms) on Ampere -> loop-reorder did NOT cut HBM (x ~1MB/batch already L2-resident); yellow flag, Turing tl.dot scheduling differs so T4 still votes
verdict: AWAIT T4 `bench.py --compile router` — held-out triangulation ref vs tldot vs readonce vs compiled

findings: readonce REFUTED (loop-reorder wrecks tl.dot on Turing, fwd 0.33x) -> REMOVED
our tl.dot conv ~2.5x slower than cuDNN (fwd 862us vs 296us; bwd dx+dw 1610us vs cuDNN convbwd 643us) -> stop fighting cuDNN
ref bwd BEATS compiled 1.20x (uncompiled cuDNN convbwd leaner than inductor bwd)
aten::topk = ~295us + gather 216us FLAT tax on EVERY path incl compiled -> the fuseable edge
verdict: pivot to cudnn backend = cuDNN conv (autograd bwd) + fused top-k epilogue

engine: novel(structural edge: fuse the native op compile can't)
change: NEW cudnn backend: F.conv1d (cuDNN, autograd -> convolution_backward) + FusedTop2Epilogue (one Triton kernel: sigmoid+bias+top-k argmax+unbiased gather, BLOCK_N=128 rows/program) replacing native topk+gather+sigmoid; bwd glue (scatter+sigmoid') torch. Combines ref's 1.20x cuDNN bwd + topk elimination. readonce removed.
verdict: AWAIT T4 `bench.py --compile router` — expect fwd up (topk killed) + bwd ~1.20x (ref-proven) -> fwd+bwd >1.0x = first conv-router WIN
cudnn = best backend, TIES compiled (0.97x ~ noise of 1.0x; baseline swung 0.44-1.0x), exact grads, beats tldot by 33%. NOT a clear speed win.
ceiling_reason: our Triton conv kernel (818us) ~= compiled WHOLE forward (821us); cuDNN owns the conv (inductor punts to it too); bwd is same convolution_backward on all paths (tie by construction). topk was the only fuseable seam and we took it.

change: cudnn: F.pad -> F.conv1d(padding=K-1)[...,:S] (causal, kills the explicit 16.8MB pad aten::copy_); set default backend = cudnn
verdict: marginal fwd nudge; CONVERGED — ship cudnn as default. AWAIT optional T4 confirm

trigger: T4: cudnn fwd 1.15x WIN (topk fused away) but bwd 0.83x (same convolution_backward as compiled + ~700us unfused glue: x->contig copied twice, casts, transposes)
change: custom-backward Function: fwd saves contiguous xc ONCE (reused in bwd, was copied 2x by autograd); bwd calls torch.ops.aten.convolution_backward directly on xc + fused _router_epilogue_bwd_kernel (sigmoid'+scatter in one, replaces sigmoid+zeros+scatter_add+2mul+cast). Removed FusedTop2Epilogue. tldot/cublas off the T4 sweep (ref+cudnn only).
verdict: AWAIT T4: fwd already 1.15x; if merged bwd clears ~1.0x -> first OVERALL conv-router WIN

result: FIRST OVERALL CONV-ROUTER WIN — merged backward flipped bwd 0.83->1.16x
next_lever: the 482us cuDNN layout transposes are a DOUBLE-transpose: x is natively (B,S,H)=NHWC, we force NCHW-contiguous, cuDNN converts back to NHWC for its *_nhwc kernel. cudnn_cl feeds channels-last to skip them (+saves 16.8MB copy).
cudnn_cl_local_ampere: 1.19x (REGRESSED vs cudnn 1.39x) — arch-dependent yellow flag; T4 must decide (T4 HAS the explicit transposes Ampere lacks)
verdict: A/B cudnn vs cudnn_cl on T4; champion cudnn protected

finding: channels-last did NOT remove cuDNN's nchwToNhwc transposes (still 6.31ms, == cudnn's 6.31ms); strided input ADDED copies (copy_ 13.2->20.0ms) + slowed convolution_backward (613->948us). cuDNN copies to its own layout regardless of input layout. The 482us transpose tax is NOT removable via cuDNN.
action: DROP cudnn_cl, restore clean cudnn champion (1.12x, unchanged). Champion was protected throughout (separate backend) -> zero regression to the shipped win.
verdict: cudnn 1.12x stands. Next lever past it = transpose-free bandwidth-optimal Triton conv (hard rewrite), nothing else in cuDNN-land helps.

change: transpose-free forward conv folding the K taps into ONE K*H=2048 contraction (_conv_router_fwd_merged_kernel + W2=weight.permute(0,2,1).reshape(E,K*H)) -> fatter/fewer tl.dots vs tldot's K skinny dots of 512. New tlconv backend (FusedConvRouterMerged); reuses transpose-free dx/dw backward. cudnn champion untouched (separate backend, A/B). Also: bench warms autotune before the correctness check (cold-cache false-CHECK fix).
correctness: forward logits IDENTICAL to tldot (abs 0.0, fp16+fp32); isolated grad PASS (gx 3e-5, gw 0.011); first cold-run showed false CHECK (autotune-during-check artifact, fixed by warm-up)
verdict: AWAIT T4 A/B: does merged-contraction Triton conv FWD beat cudnn (the tldot fwd was 818us/15x off floor on T4)? cudnn 1.12x is the bar.

tlconv: CRASHED on T4: OutOfResources shared memory Required 81920 > 65536. Merged-kernel autotune picked a big-BLOCK_C tile (128x512 fp16 = 128KB) > T4 64KB SRAM.
fix: pruned merged configs to BLOCK_C<=128 (all <40KB SRAM); wrapped bench backend loop in try/except so a crash can't abort the sweep. NOTE: shipped cudnn + BiBo integration unaffected (cudnn epilogue is tiny-SRAM).
implication: T4 64KB SRAM caps BLOCK_C ~128 -> merged contraction can't go fat -> expect tlconv ~= tldot (0.73x) = LOSE. AWAIT clean T4 to confirm/refute.
